description: Unlock the full power of the CPU by running tasks concurrently using Threads, Tasks, and Async/Await.

slides.title: Doing Two Things at Once 🤹
slides.content:
# Multithreading

By default, code runs line-by-line on a single thread (Main Thread).

**Multithreading** allows you to run code in parallel.
- **Pros:** Responsiveness (UI doesn't freeze), Performance.
- **Cons:** Complexity (Race conditions).

slides.title: The Thread Class
Thread Safety
Safety Check
slides.content: Which keyword prevents multiple threads from executing a code block simultaneously?

Async and Await
Full Example: Baking
Async Check
slides.content: Which keyword MUST be used in the function signature if you want to use `await` inside it?

slides.title: Summary
slides.content:
# Recap: Concurrency 🎓

Make your apps fly!

## Key Takeaways
- **Thread:** Low-level parallel execution.
- **Lock:** Protects shared data.
- **Task:** Higher-level abstraction (TPL).
- **Async/Await:** Non-blocking wait. Essential for modern apps.

Next, we'll finish with **WPF**, exploring how C# powers Windows Desktop Applications!
